event: Paleolithic hominins inhabit the Korean Peninsula, leaving stone tools at sites such as Jeongok-ri.
Neolithic Jeulmun pottery culture emerges with settled fishing, foraging, and comb-pattern ceramics.
Traditional founding date of Gojoseon by the legendary Dangun, Korea's first recorded kingdom.
Mumun pottery period begins, marking the spread of rice agriculture and bronze tools.
Iron Age technology spreads across the peninsula and Gojoseon develops as a regional power.
Han China conquers Wiman Joseon and establishes the Four Commanderies on the northern peninsula.
Silla is traditionally founded in the southeast under Bak Hyeokgeose.
Goguryeo is traditionally founded by Jumong in the north.
Baekje is traditionally founded by Onjo in the southwest, beginning the Three Kingdoms era.
Goguryeo conquers the Lelang commandery, expelling Chinese rule from the peninsula.
Buddhism is officially introduced to Goguryeo and a Confucian academy is established.
Goguryeo moves its capital to Pyongyang under King Jangsu at the height of its power.
Silla, allied with Tang China, conquers the kingdom of Baekje.
Silla and Tang forces destroy Goguryeo, leaving Silla dominant.
Silla expels the Tang and unifies most of the peninsula as Unified Silla.
Balhae is founded in former Goguryeo lands, beginning the Northern and Southern States period.
Wang Geon founds the Goryeo dynasty, from which the name Korea derives.
Goryeo absorbs Silla, completing reunification of the peninsula.
Goryeo institutes a Confucian civil-service examination system to recruit officials.
Goryeo decisively repels the Khitan Liao invasions at the Battle of Gwiju.
The Mongol invasions begin, eventually reducing Goryeo to a vassal of the Yuan.
Goryeo produces books with movable metal type, predating Gutenberg in Europe.
The Tripitaka Koreana woodblocks are completed during the Mongol invasions.
Yi Seong-gye founds the Joseon dynasty, which would rule Korea for over five centuries.
Joseon moves its capital to Hanyang, modern-day Seoul.
King Sejong the Great promulgates the Hangul alphabet to give Koreans a native writing system.
Japan invades in the Imjin War; Admiral Yi Sun-sin's turtle ships repel the naval assault.
The Qing invasion forces Joseon into a tributary relationship with Manchu China.
Korea repels a French naval expedition, reinforcing its isolationist policy.
The Treaty of Ganghwa with Japan opens Korean ports and ends seclusion.
The Donghak Peasant Revolution and the Gabo Reforms upend the old order amid Sino-Japanese rivalry.
King Gojong proclaims the Korean Empire, asserting independence and modernization.
The Eulsa Treaty makes Korea a protectorate of Japan after the Russo-Japanese War.
Japan formally annexes Korea, beginning 35 years of colonial rule.
The March First Movement sparks nationwide independence protests and a provisional government forms in Shanghai.
Japan's surrender ends colonial rule, and the peninsula is divided at the 38th parallel by the US and USSR.
The Republic of Korea is established in the south with Syngman Rhee as first president.
North Korea invades the south, igniting the Korean War.
An armistice halts the Korean War, leaving the peninsula divided along the DMZ.
The April Revolution forces Syngman Rhee from power after rigged elections.
Park Chung-hee seizes power in a military coup, launching state-led industrialization.
South Korea normalizes diplomatic relations with Japan.
President Park Chung-hee is assassinated, ending his authoritarian rule.
The Gwangju Uprising is violently suppressed by Chun Doo-hwan's regime.
The June Democratic Struggle wins direct presidential elections and democratization.
Seoul hosts the Summer Olympics, showcasing South Korea's rapid development.
South Korea is admitted to the United Nations alongside North Korea.
The Asian financial crisis triggers an IMF bailout and sweeping economic reforms.
South Korea co-hosts the FIFA World Cup and reaches the semifinals.
South Korea wins international praise for its rapid, tech-driven response to the COVID-19 pandemic.
UNCTAD reclassifies South Korea as a developed economy, capping its rise as a global cultural and tech power.
